messages.content:
The Eiffel Tower is a world-famous iron lattice tower located on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. Named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower, it was constructed between 1887 and 1889 as the entrance arch for the 1889 World's Fair, held to commemorate the centennial of the French Revolution. The tower stands approximately 300 meters tall (about 984 feet), making it the tallest man-made structure in the world at the time of its completion until the Chrysler Building was finished in New York City in 1930.

The Eiffel Tower has become one of the most recognizable structures globally and is a prominent symbol of France and Paris. It attracts millions of visitors each year, making it one of the most visited paid monuments in the world. The design and engineering of the tower were initially met with some resistance, but it has since been celebrated for its aesthetic and technical achievements.

The tower has three levels accessible to the public, with restaurants on the first two levels and an observation deck on the third level, offering panoramic views of the city. It continues to be a significant cultural icon and a major tourist attraction in Paris.
